title: Single-cell RNA-sequencing
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"6jtbo","text":"Protocol for preparing and sequencing scRNA libraries on Illumina NextSeq 500. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Load 10,000 cells from each sample into a separate channel of a Chromium Next GEM Chip A Single Cell Kit (10X Genomics, 120236) for use in the Chromium Controller (microfuidic based single-cell capture, 10x Genomics, 1000171, https://www.10xgenomics.com/instruments/chromium-controller )

Partition cells into nanoliter scale Gel Beads in emulsions (GEMs) and lyse cells using the Single Cell 3' Chip V3.1 GEM (10X Genomics, 1000121) 10x Genomics Single Cell 3′ Chip V3.1 GEM, Library and Gel Bead Kit
Reverse transcribe RNA and amplify using 8 cycles of PCR
Prepare the libraries using Chromium Single Cell 3' Library & Gel Bead Kit (Library & Gel Bead Kit, 10X Genomics, 120267)
Include between 15-20 ng of cDNA in the solution. Amplify using 14 rounds of PCR amplification
Sequence the libraries on NextSeq 500 System (Sequencer, Illumina, *********, https://www.illumina.com/systems/sequencing-platforms/nextseq.html ) .
creator: Luisa Cusick
